step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the selection of option B (Eisenmenger syndrome). Eisenmenger syndrome significantly impairs pulmonary vascular resistance and leads to right-to-left shunting, making pregnancy extremely risky. The associated high morbidity and mortality rates justify the urgency of performing a Cesarean Section (LSCS) immediately upon diagnosis to protect the mother’s health. Options A through D either have lower risk profiles or can be managed differently depending on specific circumstances; hence they lack the compelling necessity seen in Eisenmenger syndrome where delaying surgery could lead to fatal consequences.
Final
